title: Client Conflict Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"i1ouc","text":"Watauga Veterinary Hospital recognizes that in the practice environment there is the potential for conflict to arise between practice team members and clients. Conflicts can arise for a wide variety of reasons and may take many forms. Conflict is not always harmful in that if handled appropriately it can provide a basis for a positive change in the practice. The goal of the conflict resolution protocol is to ensure that conflicts, or potential conflicts, are identified and consistently addressed, in a manner that is fair to all parties involved and provides for the best resolution for the practice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"lmpig","text":"All practice team members are responsible for identifying and trying to resolve conflicts, however, when the individuals involved cannot resolve the issue then the procedure will be to first discuss the issue with the shift leader, practice manager, and finally the medical director. The medical director will have final responsibility for ensuring that proper procedures are followed and for the ultimate disposition of individual conflicts. Address conflicts as soon as possible, remove clients from general waiting area to minimize conflict, communicate well and in writing if necessary.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Current DVM on staff at the time of conflict
Speak in a calm manner, listen to client concerns, address each concern, be confident of Watauga Veterinary Hospital's Protocols and communicate our guidelines effectively.

Client verbal abuse directed towards any team member is not acceptable and it is appropriate to demand the client discontinue verbal abuse and remove yourself from the environment. In cases of physical abuse, contact the police immediately and attempt visual documentation.
If you would like more training, please see your practice manager.
creator: Jeanine Pierce
